blocks.content: You already know how to create variables.
For example, we can create a variable named `age` and store the number 25 in it.
When a program prints information to the console, we do not have to write the number itself.
Instead, we can write the **name of the variable**, and the program will print the value stored inside it.
`int age = 25;` creates a variable named `age` and stores the number 25 in it.
`Console.WriteLine(age);` prints the value of the variable.

The program already knows that the variable `age` contains the number 25, so the console will show:

25
It is important to see the difference between a variable and text.

If we write `Console.WriteLine(age);`, the program prints the value of the variable.

If we write `Console.WriteLine("age");`, the program prints the text "age".
Retype this code and check it.

Create a variable `score` of type `int` and assign the value 100.

Then print this variable using `Console.WriteLine`.
